titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/98

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=714 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:18:15.287203+00 | translation moa=715 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:18:17.527927+00

div.p:
Willelmus dei gratia Rex Scottorum . episcopis Abbatibus Comitis Baronibus Iusticiis Vicecomitibus prepositis ministris et omnibus probis
hominibus tocius terre sue clericis et laicis Salutem . Sciant presentes et futuri me concessisse et hac carta mea
confirmasse . deo et eccelsie sancte marie de melros . et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus illam quietam
clamationem quam Alanus filius Walteri dapifer meus eisdem monachis in presentia et plena curia
mea fecit . de tota calumpnia et omni iure quod predictus alanus aut heredes sui habuerunt uel
unquam habere poterunt de pastura ex occidentali parte de led’ . Quare uolo et precipio ut prefati
monachi de melros prenominatam pasturam teneant in perpetuum et habeant liberam
et quietam de omni calumpnia de prefato Alano et heredibus suis ita libere et quiete plenarie et
honorifice sicut carta predicti Alani filii Walteri dapiferi mei testatur . Testibus Iocelino episcopo glasg .
Iohanne episcopo Dunkeld’ . Comite dauid fratre meo . Comite Dunecano . Comite Gileb’ . Willelmo de moru’ constabulario . Willelmo
de lindes’ . Malc’ filio comitis Dunecani . Walter de Berkeley . Roberto de Berkeley . Henrico de Graham . Apud Edenburg

William by the grace of God king of the Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting. Let present and future know me to have granted, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God there, the quitclaim which Alan son of Walter my steward made to the same monks in my presence and full court, of the whole claim and every right which the said Alan or his heirs have had or ever could have had in the pasture on the western side of the Leader. Wherefore I will and instruct that the said monks of Melrose should hold the named land for ever and have it free and quit from every claim from the said Alan and his heirs, as freely and quitly, fully and honourably, as the charter of the said Alan son of Walter my steward bears witness. As witnesses, Jocelin bishop of Glasgow, John bishop of Dunkeld, Earl David my brother, Earl Duncan, Earl Gilbert, William de Morvillethe constable, William of Lindsay, Malcolm son of Earl Duncan, Walter of Berkeley, Robert of Berkeley, Henry of Graham. At Edinburgh.
